content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the retry/timeout hardening changes against `origin/master`. The direct process path looks substantially improved, but two correctness gaps remain in the tmux execution path: exit-127 is still retryable there, and the new service-level timeout path bypasses `PreparedCommand` cleanup/preservation.

## Amendments

1. src/adapters/tmux.rs:564-585 still turns every non-zero backend exit into `FailureClass::TransportFailure`. That means `execution.mode = "tmux"` does not get the new exit-127 handling that was added in src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1460-1469, so a fatal "command not found" failure is still retried in tmux mode. The acceptance criteria call out exit 127 as terminal, so the tmux adapter needs the same classification logic instead of hard-coding `TransportFailure`.
   Rationale: The retry-policy hardening is incomplete for one supported execution mode. In tmux mode the system can still burn all five retries on a fatal missing-binary failure.
2. src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:223-228 now makes the service timeout the canonical timeout for tmux, but src/adapters/tmux.rs:519-598 only runs `prepared.cleanup()` / `prepared.finish()` after `wait_for_session_exit()` returns. When the service timeout fires, that future is aborted before those lines run, and src/adapters/tmux.rs:601-658 only cleans up the tmux session itself. As a result, Codex/OpenRouter tmux timeouts leak the `PreparedCommand` temp files described in src/adapters/process_backend.rs:55-90 and skip failed-artifact preservation entirely.
   Rationale: Timeouts are now enforced, but the tmux path still loses cleanup and diagnostics on that path. Repeated timeouts will accumulate stale `runtime/temp` files and make timed-out invocations harder to inspect.
